---
title: "Kaluza Workflow Template"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Kaluza Workflow Template}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

# Standard Kaluza-to-Gated-FCS Workflow

This template documents the recurring pattern used across studies when processing
Kaluza cytometry data. Copy and adapt the relevant sections for your project
script — the building blocks come from `cytobench`; the orchestration stays in
your script where it belongs.

## Typical stages

```
read Kaluza  -->  compensate  -->  rename channels  -->  save compensated
     -->  gate subpopulations  -->  export gated  -->  QC plots
```

Not every study needs every stage. Skip what doesn't apply.

---

## 1. Read

```r
# Read Kaluza protocols + FCS data
protocols <- sapply(analysis_dirs, simplify = FALSE, cytoKal::read_protocol)

# Dataset 1: tiny read, used only as a naming reference
ds1 <- cytoKal::read_analysis_fcs(
    analysis_path,
    do_compensation = FALSE, compensation_autofluorescence = FALSE,
    af_absolute = FALSE,
    read_fcs_arguments = list(dataset = 1, which.lines = 1:3),
    first_n = 1
)

# Dataset 2: full events, the real data
ds2 <- cytoKal::read_analysis_fcs(
    analysis_path,
    do_compensation = FALSE, compensation_autofluorescence = FALSE,
    af_absolute = FALSE,
    read_fcs_arguments = list(dataset = 2)
)
```

## 2. Compensate

```r
spillovers      <- lapply(protocol[["spillover_AF"]], \(x) x$spillover_mat)
autofluorescence <- lapply(protocol[["spillover_AF"]], \(x) x$autofluorescence_proportion)

ds2_compensated <- sapply(names(ds2), simplify = FALSE, function(sample_x) {
    cytobench::compensation_autofluorescence_kaluza(
        flowframe       = ds2[[sample_x]],
        spillmat        = spillovers[[sample_x]],
        autofluorescence = autofluorescence[[sample_x]],
        calculate_absolute_values_from_ff = TRUE
    )
})
```

## 3. Rename channels (optional)

Use `rename_by_ds1_position()` when dataset-2 channels need the human-readable
names from dataset 1. This is a **positional** rename — it assumes channels are
in the same order in both datasets. If your panel re-ordered channels in Kaluza,
use a name-based mapping instead.

```r
ds2_renamed <- lapply(ds2_compensated, function(ff) {
    cytobench::rename_by_ds1_position(ff, ds1_reference = ds1[[1]])
})

# Audit the mapping
name_map <- cytobench::build_name_map(
    ds1_reference  = ds1[[1]],
    ds2_original   = ds2[[1]],
    ds2_renamed    = ds2_renamed[[1]]
)
data.table::fwrite(name_map, file.path(outdir, "name_maps.csv"))
```

## 4. Save compensated FCS

```r
cytobench::save_ff_list(ds2_renamed, outdir = file.path("res", "01_compensated", analysis_name))
```

## 5. Gate

```r
gated <- cytobench::gate_cells(
    flowset    = flowCore::flowSet(ds2_renamed),
    gatingset  = protocol$gatingsets,
    gatename   = "/Singlets/CD45+",
    return_x   = "gatinghierarchy"
)

# Extract gated flowFrames
gated_ff_list <- lapply(gated$flowset_gated, function(x) {
    tmp <- flowWorkspace::gs_pop_get_data(x, "/Singlets/CD45+")
    tmp <- flowWorkspace::realize_view(tmp)
    flowWorkspace::cytoframe_to_flowFrame(tmp[[1]])
})
```

## 6. Export gated FCS

```r
cytobench::save_ff_list(gated_ff_list, outdir = file.path(outdir, "fcs"))
```

## 7. QC plots

```r
plots <- lapply(names(gated$flowset_gated), function(x) {
    cytobench::plot_gating_simple(
        gated$flowset_gated[[x]][[1]],
        transformlist = transformlist,
        populations   = c("/Singlets/CD45+")
    )[[1]] + ggplot2::ggtitle(x)
})

pdf(file.path(outdir, "plots", "gating_overview.pdf"), width = 10, height = 7)
print(plots)
dev.off()
```

---

## Key `cytobench` helpers used

| Function | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `compensation_autofluorescence_kaluza()` | Compensate + AF correction |
| `rename_by_ds1_position()` | Positional channel rename from ds1 reference |
| `build_name_map()` | Audit rename mapping |
| `save_ff_list()` | Write a named list of flowFrames to FCS files |
| `gate_cells()` | Apply gating sets to a flowSet |
| `plot_gating_simple()` | Quick gating QC plots |

## What stays in your script

- Paths, directory layout, file discovery
- Transform definitions (cofactors vary per cytometer/panel)
- Which gate to extract (study-specific)
- Any sanity checks (e.g. comparing to a Kaluza export)
- Post-gating analyses (pseudorelativisation, clustering, etc.)
- Custom plot layouts (patchwork arrangements, etc.)
